timeline.label: Space Force terminated SCAR OTA for convenience after January stop-work; triggered ~$241M goodwill impairment in Space reporting unit
Follow-on equity offering priced to fund growth and balance-sheet flexibility post-BlueHalo
Closed BlueHalo acquisition; issued ~17.4M shares to seller; created SCDE segment and added ~$618M annual revenue run-rate
Entered new credit agreement (term loan and revolver) to refinance BlueHalo debt and fund transaction costs
Announced agreement to acquire BlueHalo, expanding from UAS/LMS into space, cyber, directed energy, and IAMD
Acquired Tomahawk Robotics (robotic control / AI-enabled comms); later leadership integrated into AV operating team
Switchblade loitering munitions demand accelerated with Ukraine conflict; Ukraine was 38% of FY2024 revenue before normalizing lower
Reincorporated in Delaware; public defense-tech platform built around small UAS and tactical precision strike

industry.structure: U.S. and allied defense primes and specialists compete for DoD unmanned systems, loitering munitions, space, cyber, and missile-defense contracts. Switchblade holds a leading LMS niche but faces entrants including Anduril, RTX, and drone specialists.
industry.share_shift: AVAV gained share in tactical LMS through Switchblade scale-up and doubled revenue via BlueHalo; Ukraine-driven UxS sales normalized lower while domestic LMS resupply grew.
industry.trend: Higher defense outlays, attritable munitions, and autonomous systems favor AVAV's portfolio, but integration risk, program cancellations (SCAR), and intangible amortization weigh on near-term owner cash.
